title: Danta Chikitsa — Dental / Oral Health Proforma
subtitle: Specialty Clinic — Shalakya Tantra / Oral Medicine (Mukha Roga / Danta Roga)

fields.content: 🌿 Danta Chikitsa Sutra: Gandusha (oil pulling with Tila taila or Triphala decoction — 5–10 min daily) is the most important Ayurveda Mukha Rogahar. For Sheetada (gingivitis): Pratisarana with Saindhava + Madhu. For Krimidanta (caries): Khadira/Karanja Kashaya rinses. Dantadhavana (tooth cleaning) with Neem/Khadira twigs — mandatory Dinacharya.

⚠ Oral Cancer Red Flags — refer immediately: White/red patch (Leucoplakia/Erythroplakia) not resolving in 2 weeks. Ulcer >2 weeks that does not heal. Submucosal fibrosis (OSMF — betel nut users). Trismus with oral lesion. Any lump on tongue/floor of mouth. These need biopsy before treatment.

fields.label: Affected Tooth / Teeth (FDI notation)
fields.placeholder: e.g. 16, 36, 11 — use FDI two-digit system (11–18 upper right, 21–28 upper left, 31–38 lower left, 41–48 lower right)

fields.label: Gingival Appearance
options: Healthy — pink, firm, stippled (Swastha Danta Mula)

fields.label: TMJ (Hanusandhi)
options: Pain on palpation of TMJ / masseter

fields.label: Mukha Daurgandhya (Bad breath) Cause
options: Systemic (DM — sweet; Uraemia — fishy; Liver failure — fetor)

fields.label: Ayurveda Treatment Notes
fields.placeholder: Gandusha (oil pulling — Tila taila / Triphala), Dantadhavana (tooth powder — Triphala, charcoal), Pratisarana (gum massage with Saindhava + honey for Sheetada), local Lepa for abscess, Nasya for TMJ pain…

fields.label: Radiograph Findings
fields.placeholder: e.g. Periapical radiolucency 36 (abscess), caries 16 approaching pulp, bone loss generalised 30%

exam_guide.title: Danta Chikitsa / Oral Medicine — Examination Protocol

steps.title: Extra-oral Inspection (Bahya Mukha Pariksha)
items: Facial symmetry — swelling, asymmetry (abscess / cellulitis / parotid)
Skin — sinus tracts / fistulae on cheek / chin (chronic dental abscess)
Lymph nodes — submandibular, cervical (infected teeth drain here)
Lips — colour, dryness, lesions, angular cheilitis
TMJ palpation — lateral pole of condyle (just anterior to tragus) + masseter muscles

steps.title: Intra-oral Inspection (Mukha Pariksha)
items: Soft tissues first — lips, cheek, tongue, floor of mouth, palate, oropharynx
LOOK for red/white patches (pre-malignant), ulcers, swellings before touching
Saliva assessment — pooling normal; xerostomia = minimal (medication / Sjögren's)
Occlusion — ask patient to bite together; observe class + crossbite + open bite
Oral hygiene — plaque index, staining, calculus deposits

steps.title: Tooth Examination (Danta Pariksha)
items: Explorer probe — tactile caries detection (soft/sticky dentine vs hard enamel)
Percussion test — tap with mirror handle; vertical pain = periapical inflammation; horizontal = PDL disease
Mobility test — push tooth faciolingually; grade 0–III
Probing — BPE pocket depth at 6 sites per tooth if periodontal disease suspected
Transillumination — detect cracks (CEREC / fibre-optic light from lingual side)

steps.title: Gandusha (Mukha Rasa Pariksha — Ayurveda)
items: Inspect tongue coating — white/thick = Ama/Kapha; red/dry = Pitta; brown = Vata
Smell (Rasa/Sparsha) — fetor = dental/systemic; fruity = DM (Madhumeha)
Jivha Sparsha — rough/dry (Vata), smooth/wet (Kapha), cracked/hot (Pitta)
Talu (palate) — Kaphaja lesions tend here (Kapalika, Rohini, Putimamsaka)
Ostha — dryness suggests Vataja Mukha Roga; bleeding = Pittaja

steps.title: TMJ Assessment (Hanusandhi Pariksha)
items: Mouth opening — normal ≥35mm (three finger breadths); <25mm = limited = trismus or ankylosis
Deviation on opening — towards affected side (disc displacement / masseter spasm)
Auscultation — stethoscope over TMJ during opening: clicking (disc displacement), crepitus (osteoarthritis)
Muscle palpation — masseter (extraoral), medial pterygoid (intraoral / pharynx), temporalis (temple)
